question_text: It takes 334,000 J of energy to melt 1 kg of ice at 0°C. How much energy is released when 500 g of water freezes at 0°C?
explanation: The latent heat of fusion is 334,000 J/kg. For 500 g (0.5 kg), the energy is 334,000 × 0.5 = 167,000 J. Freezing releases the same amount of energy that melting absorbs.
